debug_info.goal:
ι : Type
oSpec : OracleSpec ι
StmtIn StmtOut WitIn WitOut : Type
n : ℕ
pSpec : ProtocolSpec n
stmt : StmtIn
wit : WitIn
prover : Prover oSpec StmtIn WitIn StmtOut WitOut pSpec
⊢ Prod.fst <$>
      ((simulateQ loggingOracle).toFun (pSpec.FullTranscript × StmtOut × WitOut)
          (@Prover.run ι oSpec StmtIn StmtOut WitIn WitOut n pSpec stmt wit prover)).run =
    @Prover.run ι oSpec StmtIn StmtOut WitIn WitOut n pSpec stmt wit prover

failed_attempts:
have h₄ := h₁ (prover.run stmt wit)
    exact h₄
  
  exact h₂

<;> simp_all [simulq_toFun_eq, compose_eq, Prod.fst]
  <;> simp_all [run]
  <;> simp_all [simulq_toFun_eq, compose_eq, Prod.fst]

<;>
  refine' Eq.symm (Eq.symm (by
    -- Use the fact that the first projection of the product type is the same as the original value.
    simp [simulateQ_bind_fst, simulateQ_pure]
  ))
  <;>
  rfl

<;>
  (try
    {
      simp [ simulateQ_bind, ih]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    })
  <;>
  (try aesop)
  <;>
  (try
    {
      simp_all [ simulateQ_bind, ih]
      <;>
      aesop
    })
  <;>
  aesop

<;>
  simp_all [simulaterQ_run]
  <;>
  try { aesop }
  <;>
  try {
    induction prover using Prover.induction with
    | intro r =>
      intro
      simp_all
      <;>
      aesop
    | step j n ih =>
      intro
      simp_all
      <;>
      aesop
  }
  <;>
  aesop

<;>
  have h₁ : Prod.fst <$> ( ( ( @simulatesQ loggingOracle (O := (oSpec + (challengeOracleInterface pSpec).spec)) ( ( (prover.run stmt wit))
      ) ) ).run ) = (prover.run stmt wit) := by
    have h₂ : Prod.fst <$> ( ( (@simulatesQ loggingOracle (O := (oSpec + (challengeOracleInterface pSpec).spec)) ( ( (prover.run stmt wit)) ) ) ).run ) = ( (prover.run stmt wit)) := by
      have h₃ : Prod.fst <$> ( ( (@simulatesQ loggingOracle (O := (oSpec + (challengeOracleInterface pSpec).spec)) ( ( (prover.run stmt wit)) ) ) ).run ) = ( (prover.run stmt wit)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the first projection of the simulated computation is the original computation
        -- This is a direct consequence of how simulateQ is defined
        -- In particular, the first component of the result is the same as the original computation's result
        apply Eq.symm
        apply Eq.symm
        apply Eq.symm
        have h₄ := (@simulatesQ_correctness (O := (oSpec + (challengeOracleInterface pSpec).spec)) ( (prover.run stmt wit) ) )
        simp [h₄]
        <;>
        simp_all [Comp]
        <;>
        aesop
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  <;>
  simp_all [runWithLog]
  <;>
  aesop

<;>
    simp_all [Finset.card_eq_zero]
    <;>
    ext₁ x <;>
    simp_all [Finset.card_eq_zero]
    <;>
    aesop
  apply h_main

<;>
  simp_all [ simulateQ, Prod.fst_map, bind_map, map_map]
  <;>
  rfl

<;>
  simp_all [simulateQ, Q1]
  <;>
  simp_all [bind, pure, m, l, liftComp, Prod.fst]
  <;>
  rfl

<;>
    rfl
  
  have h₂ : Prod.fst <$> ( (simulateQ loggingOracle (prover.run stmt wit)).run ) = prover.run stmt wit := by
    apply simulateQ_map_fst
  
  have h₃ : Prod.fst <$> prover.runWithLog stmt wit = prover.run stmt wit := by
    rw [h₁]
    rw [h₂]
  
  apply h₃

<;>
  (try simp_all [sim, simulateQ, run, connection, hybridConnectionRun, Prod.comp])
  <;>
  rfl

<;>
  (try simp_all [run, runToRound, Fin.last, Fin.succ_zero_eq_one, Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try rfl) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log]) <;>
  (try simp_all [simulable, sim, sim_log])
